source_description: spec number: 011, slug: 011-alps-parser-generator, friendly name: ALPS Parser and Generator, source: GitHub issue #97, target branch: master, description: Parse ALPS JSON/XML documents into a typed F# representation, and generate ALPS JSON from StateMachineMetadata. This enables ALPS as both an input format (for LLM-assisted code scaffolding) and an output format (from compiled Frank.Statecharts assemblies). Parser and generator share the same AST types. Parent issue: #57. Priority: P2. Depends on: #87 (core runtime — StateMachineMetadata types). Parallel with: #98 (SCXML), #100 (smcat), #90 (WSD Parser). Deliverables: (1) ALPS JSON parser - Deserialize ALPS JSON into typed F# model, (2) ALPS XML parser - Deserialize ALPS XML into typed F# model, (3) ALPS to StateMachineMetadata mapper - Map ALPS descriptors to StateMachineMetadata (best-effort, lossy due to ALPS intentional omission of workflow topology), (4) ALPS JSON generator - Generate ALPS JSON from StateMachineMetadata with single-valued rt limitation requiring one descriptor per conditional target (D-006) and guard labels via ext elements, (5) Roundtrip tests - Parse ALPS to metadata to generate ALPS verify consistency, (6) Golden file tests - Compare generated output against the ALPS examples from #57. Known Limitations: ALPS cannot distinguish PUT from DELETE (both idempotent), ALPS has no workflow ordering, rt is single-valued requiring multiple descriptors for conditional transitions, guard semantics are in ext elements only. CLI Integration: frank statechart import api.alps.json, frank statechart import api.alps.xml, frank statechart generate --format alps MyApp.dll. References: #57 (parent ALPS mapping rules and examples), ALPS Specification http://alps.io/spec/drafts/draft-01.html, Research decision D-006
